content.problemStatement:
Flappy Bird is on a screen with height HHH pixels (a 2D plane where the yyy-axis corresponds to the vertical direction). There are NNN vertical obstacles in the room (numbered 111 through NNN); for each valid iii, the coordinates of the endpoint of the iii-th obstacle are (xi,ai)(x_i, a_i)(xi​,ai​). There are two types of obstacles:


Type 0: A line segment between the points (xi,0)(x_i, 0)(xi​,0) and (xi,ai)(x_i, a_i)(xi​,ai​), i.e. upwards from the floor.
Type 1: A line segment between the points (xi,H)(x_i, H)(xi​,H) and (xi,ai)(x_i, a_i)(xi​,ai​), i.e. downwards from the ceiling.
For each obstacle, you need to find the number of endpoints of other obstacles (not including itself) that are visible from its endpoint. Two endpoints are visible from each other if the line segment joining them does not intersect or touch any other obstacle. Note that each obstacle has exactly one endpoint.
constraints: 1≤ai≤H−11 \le a_i \le H-11≤ai​≤H−1 for each valid iii
no two obstacles touch or intersect
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 20,00020,00020,000
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers HHH and NNN.
The following NNN lines describe the obstacles. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these lines contains three space-separated integers tit_iti​, xix_ixi​ and aia_iai​, where tit_iti​ is the type of the iii-th obstacle.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing NNN space-separated integers. For each valid iii, the iii-th of these integers should be the number of endpoints visible from the iii-th endpoint.
